Yagya Bahadur Bogati is a Nepali politician and CPN-UML member who served in Nepal's first federal parliament. He was elected from Achham-2 constituency in the 2017 general election with 21,965 votes (53.26%). He defeated Nepali Congress candidate Pushpa Bahadur Shah. In 2079 BS, CPN-UML Chairman KP Sharma Oli selected him as the candidate for Achham-2 again. He has been a consistent representative for Achham district.

Pushpa Bahadur Shah is a Nepalese politician, belonging to the Nepali Congress currently serving as a member of the 2nd Federal Parliament of Nepal. In the 2022 Nepalese general election, he won the election from Achham 2 (constituency). Previously elected to parliament in 2079 BS from Achham-2. Education: Masters (MA/MSc/MBS).

Chatur Bahadur Chand (born 25 July 1971) is former 
president of Cricket Association of Nepal (CAN)
 and a politician of the Nepali Congress. He is the current district committee chairman of Baitadi district committee of the Nepali Congress.
He was elected as sixth president of the CAN on 29 September 2019, after the International Cricket Council lifted a ban on CAN. He was once again re-elected as president for second time on 23 September 2023. Education: Bachelors (BA/BSc/BBS).

Ain Bahadur Mahar is a CPN-UML central committee member and former president of ANNFSU (All Nepal National Free Students Union), the student wing of CPN-UML. Originally from Bajhang district, he entered party politics through student activism. He was nominated to the central committee in Mangsir 2078. He contested from Bajhang in the 2079 election but was defeated by CPN-Unified Socialist candidate Bhanubhakta Joshi. In August 2025, he was arrested on cyber crime charges related to defamation allegations and was later released on bail.

Prakash Rasaili 'Snehi' is a member of the House of Representatives (Pratinidhi Sabha) and a Central Committee member of Nepali Congress. He serves on the party's Active Membership Management Committee. He announced his candidacy for Central Member position at the 14th General Convention. He has been actively involved in internal party organizational matters and has spoken out about delays in membership renewal processes.

Lal Bahadur Thapa is a Nepali politician and a member of the House of Representatives of the federal parliament of Nepal. He was elected under the first-past-the-post system representing CPN UML of the left alliance, in Bajura-1 constituency. He defeated his nearest rival, Kabiraj Pandit of Nepali Congress, by acquiring 28,065 votes to Pandit's 23,181.

Narayan Prasad Gaudel is an influential Nepali Congress leader from Banke district, known as a skilled organizer. A resident of Kohalpur, he is recognized as an honest leader with a simple lifestyle who works tirelessly for the party. He progressed through Tarun Dal, served as Regional President, District Member, District Co-Secretary, and Central Organization Department member. He has been an active member since 2036 BS and a party activist since 2048 BS. He was elected District Co-Secretary in 2059 BS and Regional President of Constituency No. 1 in 2061 BS. He is currently a Mahasamiti (National Committee) member.

Prakash Bahadur Shah (Nepali: प्रकाश बहादुर शाह) is a Nepalese politician and served as the Minister for Internal Affairs and Law and the Minister for Economic Affairs of Government of Sudurpashchim Province. Ghanashyam Pandey was the CPN-UML mayoral candidate for Tulsipur Sub-Metropolitan City. He is an expert in forestry, environment, cooperatives, and agriculture, having extensively studied green development and green economy models worldwide. He served as the Central President of the Community Forest Users Federation and was also the coordinator of the Global Community Forest Network. From a simple farmer family, he has met world leaders including former US President Barack Obama. Under his leadership, Tulsipur saw construction of a well-equipped municipal office building, Pushpalal Children's Park, greenbelts, vegetable market, and slaughterhouse.

Bhumi Prasad Tripathi started his political journey in 2036 BS through student politics. A central member of Nepali Congress, Saud previously served as the Minister of Irrigation from 25 February 2014 to 11 October 2015 in Sushil Koirala's cabinet. He is a member of the Pratinidhi Sabha from Kanchanpur 2, having won the seat in the 2022 general election.

He also served as coordinator of the CPN-UML National Convention Stage Management Committee alongside Central Member Khimlal Bhattarai. He represents Oceania region (Australia) as a convention delegate for the party.

Elected twice from Udayapur Constituency No. 1 to the Province Assembly (2), he is a former Physical Infrastructure Development Minister of the Province government. He also serves as the District President of Nepali Congress Udayapur.

Deputy PM & Defence Minister (2018-2021). Senior Vice-President of CPN-UML. Founding Central Committee member of CPN (ML) in 1980. Two-time General Secretary of CPN-UML (2009-2018). Key architect of peace process and Maoist combatant integration.

Pradeep Poudel is a Nepali Congress politician and current General Secretary elected at the 2082 BS Special Convention. He started his political career in Nepal Student Union from Amrit Campus, becoming SVVIU President in 2053 BS, Nepal Student Union Central President in 2064 BS, and Federal MP from Kathmandu-5 in 2079 BS. He became Health Minister in 2081 BS. He was a frontline youth leader in the 2063 BS People's Movement alongside Gagan Thapa and PM Girija Prasad Koirala. He led initiatives including the Mechi-Mahakali campaign against constitution-writing delays, post-earthquake relief efforts, youth entrepreneurship campaigns, and created digital apps for Nepali Congress and a plasma app for COVID patients.

Deputy PM 3 times (2015-16, 2017, 2017-18). Foreign Minister 3 times. Home Minister during King Gyanendra's controversial direct rule (2005-06) - human rights groups called for sanctions. Held 12+ ministerial portfolios since 1988. Former national footballer, ANFA President 1978-87. Lost RPP chairmanship to Lingden 2021, formed RPP-Nepal, reunified December 2025. Detained June 2025 during pro-monarchy demonstration. Contesting Kathmandu-5.

NC Senior Leader & Spokesperson. Foreign Minister 2016, Finance Minister 2023-24. PhD Economics from Southern Illinois University. Member of Constitution Drafting Committee. Brother of Ram Sharan Mahat (former Finance Minister). Author of 'Nepali Congress and Nation Building'. Controversy: Finance Minister tenure ended amid fiscal management criticism in 2024.

Hit Bahadur Tamang (born 29 May 1963) is a Nepali politician and a member of the House of Representatives of the federal parliament of Nepal. He was elected from the Nuwakot-1 constituency representing the CPN (Maoist Centre) of the Left Alliance, defeating senior Nepali Congress leader and ex-finance minister Ram Saran Mahat. Madhav Prasad Dhungel was born on Ashoj 5, 2034 BS to father Bodhraj Dhungel and mother Laxmi Dhungel. He is the eldest of three children (one son, two daughters). He passed SLC from his village school and went to Mahendra Morang Campus, Biratnagar for higher studies in Arts (English, Mathematics, Economics). He later completed BL from Nepal Law Campus and obtained an advocate's license. He served as CPN-UML Pakarvas Village Committee Secretary (2051-55) and Ramechhap District Committee member, vice-chairman, chairman (2048-2055). He is a CPN-UML leader and Youth Council President, appointed as Executive Vice President of National Youth Council. He serves as Secretary of CPN-UML Central Organization Department. He was elected unopposed from the Kathmandu Valley special open male category at the UML convention.

Tarakant Chaudhary is the unanimously elected Chairman of CPN-UML Saptari district committee. He was elected at the 9th district convention held in Agnisair Krishnasavaran Rural Municipality with a 131-member district committee. He was previously elected as a member of the Second Constituent Assembly from Saptari Constituency No. 3. After the CPN split, he became district committee chairman from Baisakh 22, 2078 BS. The committee includes Vice-President Abdul Majid Shakir, Secretary Devnath Yadav, and Assistant Secretaries Akhilesh Jha and Kusum Acharya. A 23-member advisory committee including Shankar Dev, Manju Sah, and others was also formed. Secretary Raghubir Mahaseth administered the oath of office.
